payload.description: Create a modern external OpenClaw channel plugin where each Gmail thread maps to one isolated OpenClaw session. Deliver package scaffolding, secure account configuration, Gmail API transport, polling ingress, threaded replies, tests, documentation, and package validation.
payload.title: Build Gmail-backed OpenClaw channel

payload.description: Create TypeScript ESM package metadata, manifest, build/test/lint scripts, runtime entry, CI, and pinned OpenClaw compatibility metadata. Acceptance: package installs dependencies, entry follows defineChannelPluginEntry, build and an entry-shape test pass. Verification: pnpm test; pnpm build; pnpm typecheck.
payload.title: Scaffold the modern OpenClaw plugin package

payload.description: Define strict Zod channel schemas, secret-input-aware account resolution, target parsing, and deny-by-default inbound/outbound email policy. Acceptance: named/default accounts resolve without exposing secret values; exact/domain allowlists are case-insensitive; malformed targets/config fail closed. Verification: focused config and target tests plus typecheck.
payload.title: Implement secure Gmail account configuration

payload.description: Add a narrow Gmail client boundary, bounded MIME extraction, RFC 2822/base64url construction, unread listing/get/mark-read, and send/reply operations. Acceptance: untrusted API data is validated; text/plain and safe HTML fallback parse; new sends and replies produce correct Gmail payloads and return message/thread IDs. Verification: transport tests with a fake API, build, npm audit.
payload.title: Implement Gmail API message transport

payload.description: Implement polling gateway and inbound dispatch through current runtime helpers, using Gmail thread IDs as channel peers so sessions are isolated per thread. Acceptance: same thread produces same session route; different threads differ; self/disallowed senders never dispatch; successful dispatch marks read; reply delivery stays in the source thread. Verification: gateway/inbound tests and typecheck.
payload.title: Route polled Gmail threads through OpenClaw

payload.description: Write setup/configuration documentation, limitations and security notes; pack the built plugin and inspect/install it against current OpenClaw; run full validation and pre-ship reviews. Acceptance: README explains OAuth and target grammar; npm pack contains only required artifacts; OpenClaw runtime inspection recognizes gmail; all quality gates pass. Verification: pnpm check; npm pack --dry-run; OpenClaw plugin inspection.
payload.title: Document, package, and validate the first milestone

payload.description: Prove how OpenClaw should replace a provisional mailto recipient route with the canonical Gmail thread ID returned after messages.send. Add plugin-owned mapping or propose the smallest SDK enhancement so subsequent replies continue the outbound-created session.
payload.title: Reconcile outbound-originated Gmail thread sessions

payload.description: Implement an OpenClaw setup entry and installed-app OAuth flow with PKCE, least-privilege gmail.modify scope, secret-provider storage, cancellation, and doctor diagnostics. Do not expose refresh tokens in logs or config summaries.
payload.title: Add interactive Gmail OAuth setup

payload.description: Add bounded attachment download/storage plus multi-recipient reply-all with independent allowTo checks for every To/Cc recipient. Cover MIME nesting, filename safety, size limits, and unsupported attachment behavior.
payload.title: Add Gmail attachments and reply-all

payload.description: Compare polling with History API cursors and Pub/Sub push notifications. Specify durable cursor storage, missed-history recovery, webhook authentication, operational requirements, and rate-limit behavior before implementation.
payload.title: Evaluate Gmail History API and push delivery

payload.body: Validated with pnpm check (38 tests), dependency audit, package inspection, independent review, and runtime loading against OpenClaw origin/main 10853e82fdeb4939c8f3ed0f20697790a52fb617.
First milestone complete. Enhancements for outbound session reconciliation, OAuth setup, attachments/reply-all, and push delivery remain tracked separately.
